steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: Before the blank: “The grammar of the Gujarati language includes several noun cases common to most Indo-Aryan languages” is an independent clause. After the blank: “the locative case, for instance, indicates that an action is occurring at, on, or near a particular noun” is an independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the rule for joining independent clauses
steps.content: A semicolon is the correct punctuation to join two independent clauses without a coordinating conjunction. Choice C provides this semicolon, correctly connecting the two clauses. Choices A and B use only a comma, creating a comma splice, and choice D uses a semicolon but then awkwardly inserts “such as” (a phrase that introduces examples, not independent clauses) between them. That matches choice C — C is correct.
